questions.q: The Taj Mahal is located in which Indian city?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal stands on the banks of the Yamuna river at Agra.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor built the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was commissioned by the fifth Mughal emperor, Shah Jahan.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal was built as a memorial for which person?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal is chiefly built of which material, giving it its gleaming white appearance?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is famously clad in white marble, much of it from Makrana in Rajasthan.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal is most often described as a monument symbolising what?
questions.explanation: Built for Mumtaz Mahal, the Taj Mahal is celebrated worldwide as a monument to love.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal is an example of which style of architecture?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is regarded as the supreme masterpiece of Mughal architecture.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal has been recognised internationally with which designation?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, inscribed in 1983.

questions.q: Beside which river does the Taj Mahal stand?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal overlooks the Yamuna river at Agra.

questions.q: Tall slender towers, four of them, frame the Taj Mahal. What are these called?
questions.explanation: Four minarets stand at the corners of the Taj Mahal's marble plinth.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal was completed around which year?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal complex is generally said to have been completed around 1653 CE.

questions.q: Construction of the Taj Mahal began in approximately which year, soon after Mumtaz Mahal's death?
questions.explanation: Work on the Taj Mahal began around 1632, the year after Mumtaz Mahal died in 1631.

questions.q: Mumtaz Mahal, for whom the Taj was built, died in which circumstances?
questions.explanation: Mumtaz Mahal died in 1631 giving birth to her fourteenth child, which led Shah Jahan to build the Taj.

questions.q: Besides Mumtaz Mahal, whose tomb was later placed inside the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan himself was buried beside Mumtaz Mahal inside the Taj Mahal after his death in 1666.

questions.q: The decorative inlay of semi-precious stones into the Taj's marble is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is adorned with pietra dura, called parchin kari, inlaying coloured stones into marble.

questions.q: The marble used for the Taj Mahal was famously quarried from which place in Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: The white marble of the Taj Mahal was sourced largely from Makrana in Rajasthan.

questions.q: What kind of formal garden lies in front of the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is set within a charbagh, a four-part Mughal garden divided by watercourses.

questions.q: The grand entrance gateway and the great central dome of the Taj reflect influences from which architectural traditions?
questions.explanation: The Taj blends Persian, Central Asian, and Indian Islamic design in its dome, iwan, and gateway.

questions.q: Flanking the main mausoleum are two red sandstone buildings; one is a mosque and the other is which?
questions.explanation: To balance the mosque, a matching jawab ('answer') building stands on the other side for symmetry.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal's minarets lean slightly outward; by what reason is this most commonly explained?
options: So that, if they fell, they would topple away from the tomb
questions.explanation: The minarets tilt slightly outward, and the traditional explanation is that this would make them fall away from the central mausoleum rather than onto it.

questions.q: The verses inlaid in black stone around the Taj's archways are passages from which text?
questions.explanation: Calligraphic inscriptions of Quranic verses, in black marble inlay, decorate the Taj Mahal's portals.

questions.q: The architect traditionally credited as the chief designer of the Taj Mahal is named as which?
questions.explanation: Ustad Ahmad Lahauri is traditionally regarded as the chief architect of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Which feature of the central dome of the Taj Mahal gives it its distinctive shape?
options: It is a bulbous onion (amrud) dome
questions.explanation: The Taj is crowned by a large bulbous 'onion' or amrud dome topped by a finial.

questions.q: The two tombs visible in the main chamber of the Taj Mahal are which kind?
options: Cenotaphs above the real graves in a lower crypt
questions.explanation: The chamber holds decorative cenotaphs; the true graves of Mumtaz Mahal and Shah Jahan lie in a crypt below.

questions.q: Within the symmetrical layout of the Taj Mahal, which element notably breaks the perfect symmetry?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan's larger cenotaph, added later beside Mumtaz Mahal's central one, is the only asymmetric element.

questions.q: The mosque flanking the Taj Mahal faces which direction, as required for prayer?
questions.explanation: The mosque is oriented westward toward Mecca, which is why a matching jawab was built to its east for balance.

questions.q: Mumtaz Mahal died at which place while accompanying Shah Jahan on a Deccan campaign?
questions.explanation: Mumtaz Mahal died at Burhanpur in 1631; her remains were later moved to Agra for burial in the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: The full marble-clad mausoleum building of the Taj is generally said to have been finished by about which year, before the surrounding complex?
questions.explanation: The central mausoleum was largely complete by around 1643, with the wider complex finished about a decade later.

questions.q: A popular but historically unverified legend claims Shah Jahan planned what counterpart across the river?
options: A black marble Taj for himself
questions.explanation: The 'Black Taj' legend, that Shah Jahan intended a mirror tomb in black marble, is not supported by firm evidence.

questions.q: The garden across the Yamuna, aligned with the Taj Mahal and linked to its original riverfront design, is known as which?
questions.explanation: The Mehtab Bagh ('Moonlight Garden') lies directly across the river, aligned with the Taj as part of its riverside plan.

questions.q: In the colour of its marble, the Taj Mahal appears to change subtly with what?
options: The light of different times of day
questions.explanation: The translucent marble of the Taj Mahal takes on different hues with the changing light of dawn, day, and moonlight.
